title: dryopteris subelata
intro: Dryopteris subelata, also known as the subalpine wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris subelata have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Dryopteris subelata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
